commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds Codex `personalityMode`, SOUL-aware app-server personality routing, a Codex/OpenClaw turn-context bridge, context-engine projection changes, docs, and focused Codex harness tests.

Reproducibility: yes. source-level: run `/codex resume` in a session whose workspace contains `SOUL.md`, then send a Codex turn; the PR's new mismatch check clears that binding before `thread/resume`. I did not execute it because this was a read-only review, but the current command binding and proposed lifecycle check line up directly.
commands.review_followup: A narrow automated repair can preserve manual/legacy Codex bindings and add focused regression tests without changing the broader personality policy decision.

target.title: [codex] Route native personality through SOUL context
target.body:
## Summary This PR keeps the Codex harness anchor intact while preventing Codex's named native personality overlay from outranking a user-provided OpenClaw `SOUL.md`. It combines four related pieces in one reviewable change: 1. **SOUL-aware Codex personality routing** - Adds `plugins.entries.codex.config.personalityMode`. - Defaults to `"soul_when_present"`. - When OpenClaw injects `SOUL.md` into the Codex turn context, OpenClaw sends Codex app-server `personality: "none"` for that thread. - This is a structured app-server enum, not prompt text that tells the model to "have no personality". The positive personality source remains `SOUL.md`. 2. **A narrow OpenClaw/Codex bridge** - Keeps Codex base/model/project-doc behavior in the native Codex lane. - Adds developer-level bridge wording only when OpenClaw turn context is injected. - Explains that Codex is the native execution harness, OpenClaw is the host runtime, and OpenClaw turn context is reference data rather than developer policy. - If `SOUL.md` is present, explicitly points personality/tone/agent voice at the SOUL file when compatible with higher-priority safety, privacy, tool, and current-request rules. 3. **Prompt hardening plus docs/tests** - Keeps raw `SOUL.md`, skills, and bootstrap files out of Codex developer instructions. - Projects context-engine `systemPromptAddition` into Codex turn reference context instead of Codex developer instructions. - Documents the priority model, configuration modes, and context-engine behavior. - Adds regression tests for default SOUL behavior, opt-out behavior, bridge wording, session binding, context-engine projection, and type/config parsing. 4. **CI test hardening** - Keeps `source-delivery-plan.test.ts` focused on source-delivery matching by mocking the outbound target normalizer. - Avoids loading the full channel plugin registry in a tiny unit test, which was timing out in the unrelated `checks-node-core-fast` shard. ## Why The previous fix correctly stopped raw OpenClaw workspace prompt files from being promoted into Codex developer instructions. That avoided giving user-editable files developer priority. However, it left an important edge case: if Codex's native personality overlay remained active, a custom OpenClaw `SOUL.md` could still lose personality priority to Codex's built-in friendly/pragmatic personality. That is especially awkward because OpenClaw is not only a coding agent surface, while the Codex harness still needs to preserve its coding-capable anchor and native tool discipline. This PR chooses the middle path: - Keep Codex's native harness identity. - Do not promote raw SOUL text to developer instructions. - Disable Codex's named native personality only when SOUL is present by default. - Use a bridge instruction to say where personality should come from. - Leave explicit config escape hatches for deployments that want Codex personality always on or always off. ## Prompt Priority Diagram ```mermaid flowchart TD A["Codex base/model instructions
harness identity, tools, safety"] --> B["Codex native personality enum"] B --> C["OpenClaw developer bridge
runtime + context authority"] C --> D["OpenClaw turn context
SOUL, IDENTITY, USER, MEMORY, skills"] D --> E["Current user request"] S["SOUL.md injected by OpenClaw"] --> N["Send app-server personality: none"] N --> B S --> C C --> P["Use SOUL.md for personality/tone
when compatible with higher-priority rules"] ``` Important distinction: `personality: "none"` is sent as Codex app-server configuration. It is not inserted into the model prompt as "you have no personality." The model still receives a positive developer bridge that names `SOUL.md` as the personality/tone source. ## Context-Engine Hardening Diagram ```mermaid flowchart LR CE["context-engine systemPromptAddition"] --> CTX["Codex turn reference context"] CTX --> INPUT["turn/start input"] CE -. "no longer for native Codex" .-> DEV["Codex developerInstructions"] ``` For OpenClaw-assembled prompt surfaces, context engines can still contribute to the assembled system prompt. For native Codex app-server turns, that dynamic guidance is now quoted as turn reference context so retrieval/memory hints do not outrank Codex system/developer policy. ## Configuration New Codex plugin config: ```json5 { "plugins": { "entries": { "codex": { "config": { "personalityMode": "soul_when_present" } } } } } ``` Accepted values: | Value | Behavior | | --- | --- | | `"soul_when_present"` | Default. Disable Codex's named native personality only when OpenClaw injects `SOUL.md`; use SOUL as the personality/tone reference. | | `"codex"` | Keep Codex's native personality overlay even when `SOUL.md` is present. | | `"none"` | Always send Codex `personality: "none"` for app-server threads. | ## Real behavior proof **Behavior addressed:** Native Codex app-server turns now keep Codex harness identity while routing personality priority to OpenClaw `SOUL.md` when SOUL is injected. Context-engine `systemPromptAddition` is no longer appended to Codex developer instructions. **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw source checkout on macOS, built through the real OpenClaw CLI/runtime build path at `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-prompt-surface-runtime-separation`, commit `a70753510f`. **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** ```bash pnpm openclaw --help node --input-type=module --eval ' import { s as readCodexPluginConfig, u as resolveCodexPersonalityOverride } from "./dist/config-Om76xpjW.js"; import { i as buildDeveloperInstructions, _ as projectContextEngineAssemblyForCodex } from "./dist/thread-lifecycle-C3oeDvg2.js"; const developerInstructions = buildDeveloperInstructions( { sourceReplyDeliveryMode: "automatic" }, { includeOpenClawTurnContextBridge: true, hasSoulPromptContext: true }, ); const projection = projectContextEngineAssemblyForCodex({ assembledMessages: [], originalHistoryMessages: [], prompt: "hello from real smoke", systemPromptAddition: "context-engine system", systemPromptAdditionMode: "context", }); console.log(JSON.stringify({ openclawCheckout: process.cwd(), commit: "a70753510f", defaultSoulPersonality: resolveCodexPersonalityOverride({ pluginConfig: readCodexPluginConfig({}), hasSoulPromptContext: true, }), codexModePersonality: resolveCodexPersonalityOverride({ pluginConfig: readCodexPluginConfig({ personalityMode: "codex" }), hasSoulPromptContext: true, }) ?? null, bridgeMentionsHostRuntime: developerInstructions.includes("OpenClaw is the host runtime"), bridgePointsToSoul: developerInstructions.includes("use SOUL.md from the OpenClaw turn context"), rawSoulTextInDeveloperInstructions: developerInstructions.includes("Soul voice goes here."), contextEngineDeveloperAddition: projection.developerInstructionAddition ?? null, contextEnginePromptHasReferenceHeader: projection.promptText.includes("Context-engine guidance:"), contextEnginePromptHasSystemAddition: projection.promptText.includes("context-engine system"), }, null, 2)); ' ``` **Evidence after fix:** Copied live terminal output from the built OpenClaw checkout: ```text OpenClaw 2026.5.17 (a707535) — All your chats, one OpenClaw. { "openclawCheckout": "/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-prompt-surface-runtime-separation", "commit": "a70753510f", "defaultSoulPersonality": "none", "codexModePersonality": null, "bridgeMentionsHostRuntime": true, "bridgePointsToSoul": true, "rawSoulTextInDeveloperInstructions": false, "contextEngineDeveloperAddition": null, "contextEnginePromptHasReferenceHeader": true, "contextEnginePromptHasSystemAddition": true } ``` **Observed result after fix:** The real built OpenClaw checkout reports the default SOUL path resolves to Codex `personality: "none"`, the `"codex"` config path leaves native personality unset, developer bridge text is present and points to SOUL, raw SOUL contents are absent from developer instructions, and context-engine guidance is present in turn reference context while developer-instruction addition is `null`. **What was not tested:** No live paid model request or remote Codex app-server network turn was run for this PR. The live proof exercises the built OpenClaw runtime/prompt-construction path locally; request-construction behavior is covered by focused app-server tests. ## Behavior Addressed Before: - Raw `SOUL.md` was not in developer instructions, which was good. - But Codex's named native personality overlay could still remain active above the SOUL turn context. - Context-engine `systemPromptAddition` could be appended to Codex developer instructions. After: - Raw SOUL text remains turn context, not developer policy. - The developer bridge says how to interpret OpenClaw context and when to use SOUL for voice. - Default Codex personality is neutralized only when SOUL is present. - Context-engine dynamic additions are turn reference context for native Codex. ## Real Environment Tested Checkout: `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-prompt-surface-runtime-separation` Branch: `codex/soul-personality-bridge` Commit: `a70753510f [codex] route native personality through soul context` ## Exact Steps Or Commands Run After This Patch ```bash git diff --check OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs \ extensions/codex/src/app-server/config.test.ts \ extensions/codex/src/app-server/thread-lifecycle.test.ts \ extensions/codex/src/app-server/session-binding.test.ts \ extensions/codex/src/app-server/context-engine-projection.test.ts \ extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.context-engine.test.ts \ extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs \ src/infra/outbound/source-delivery-plan.test.ts pnpm check:test-types ``` ## Evidence After Fix - Focused Vitest: `7 passed (7)` files, `292 passed (292)` tests. - Source delivery plan test is included in the focused Vitest command; local duration for that file after hardening was `279ms` in the pre-rebase run. - Type test check: `pnpm check:test-types` passed. - Whitespace check: `git diff --check` passed. ## Observed Result After Fix - Codex app-server thread start includes `personality: "none"` when SOUL is injected under the default config. - `personalityMode: "codex"` leaves native Codex personality unset even when SOUL is present. - Developer instructions contain the OpenClaw/Codex bridge and no raw SOUL contents. - Cron/lightweight bootstrap path does not add the OpenClaw context bridge or personality override. - Context-engine `systemPromptAddition` is absent from Codex developer instructions and present in turn reference context. ## What Was Not Tested - No full local repo-wide suite was run; this was intentionally kept to focused local validation plus type checks under the Lexar resource policy. - No live Codex app-server manual smoke was run in this PR turn; the change is covered by the app-server request-construction and run-attempt tests.
